response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills and leadership experience, they fundamentally lack the required specialization. Most critically, C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014-2015) versus the 8-year requirement, and there is no demonstrated background in cloud compute platform architecture or large-scale distributed infrastructure leadership. The resume shows a full-stack web/backend generalist with diverse language exposure, not the specialized cloud platform engineer needed for this mid-senior technical leadership role.
strengths: Extensive software engineering maturity with 15+ years of diverse experience across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems
Proven experience with high-performance systems (handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby) and infrastructure work (Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD)
Leadership trajectory with Staff-level titles and demonstrated mentoring capability through workshops, talks, and community contributions
concerns: Severe C++ experience gap: only ~6 months at National Instruments (2014-2015) versus the 8-year minimum requirement, with recent 10 years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript
No demonstrated experience with cloud compute platform architecture or large-scale infrastructure leadership in cloud/compute context
Career pattern shows generalist full-stack developer across multiple languages and domains rather than deep specialization in C++ systems or cloud platforms

key_factors.factor: General technical leadership and mentoring capability
